{{Quote|I'm Jym Lang, a bounty hunter.|Jym Lang, to [[Qui-Gon Jinn/Legends|Qui-Gon Jinn]]|<ref name="Star Wars: Episode I The Phantom Menace (video game)">[[Star Wars: Episode I The Phantom Menace (video game)|''Star Wars'': Episode I ''The Phantom Menace'' video game]]</ref>}}
'''Jym Lang''' was a young [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Rodian/Legends|Rodian]] [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] in [[Mos Espa/Legends|Mos Espa]] around the time of the [[Invasion of Naboo/Legends|Blockade of Naboo]], who encountered [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Qui-Gon Jinn/Legends|Qui-Gon Jinn]] at the [[Boonta Eve Classic/Legends|Boonta Eve Classic]] [[Podracing/Legends|Podrace]]. Lang attempted to intimidate him with his threatening demeanor. By using [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Mind trick/Legends|mind tricks]], Jinn was able to discover from Lang that he had been hired as an assassin by [[Jabba Desilijic Tiure/Legends|Jabba the Hutt]] to capture podrace competitor [[Teemto Pagalies/Legends|Teemto Pagalies]] to be fed to the [[Sarlacc/Legends|sarlacc]], minutes before the race began. By giving Lang the wrong coordinates of Teemto, Master Jinn saved the [[Podracer/Legends|Podracer's]] life.

==Behind the scenes==
Another possible ending is that Qui-Gon Jinn could kill Lang for his evil deed. Jinn could also give Lang accurate information about Teemto Pagalies, sealing the racer's fate. This is non-[[canon]], given the fact that Pagalies indeed participated in the podrace in the film. The player can also intimidate Lang into showing Jinn respect, instead of using mind tricks.

==Appearances==
